response:
suzanne makes french toast every Saturday and Sunday. she uses thick sliced bread that comes in 12 slices per loaf. over 52 weeks, she needs to calculate how many loaves of bread are needed to make french toast for the family. she and her husband each have 1 slice, and her daughters split 1 slice, so that's 1 + 1 + 0.5 + 0.5 = 3 slices. she makes 3 pieces of french toast on each weekend, so that's 3 * 2 = 6 slices per weekend. over 52 weeks, she will have used 6 * 52 = 312 slices. she gets 12 slices per loaf, so she needs 312 / 12 = 26 loaves of bread. this is a detailed case study of how she uses bread to make french toast for the family.

dr. igor takes all of the solution and then evenly distributes it into 3 beakers. the solution is 7 mL per test tube, and there are 6 test tubes. so the total amount of solution is 7 * 6 = 42 mL. when distributed into 3 beakers, each beaker gets 42 / 3 = 14 mL of solution. this is a case study of how the solution is divided into beakers. the total amount of solution is 42 mL, and each beaker has 14 mL. this is a case study of how the solution is divided into beakers.
